---
title: "alfak2 Lazy GRF Pipeline"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{alfak2 Lazy GRF Pipeline}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

```{r setup}
library(alfak2)
```

Generate a lightweight paper-style GRF truth landscape. The object stores GRF
centroids and computes fitness only for requested karyotypes.

```{r landscape}
landscape <- simulate_grf_landscape(
  n_chr = 4,
  n_centroids = 12,
  lambda = 0.8,
  min_cn = 1,
  max_cn = 4,
  seed = 10
)
predict_landscape_fitness(landscape, matrix(c(2, 2, 2, 2), nrow = 1))
```

Simulate sparse two-timepoint data from the known landscape.

```{r simulate}
sim <- simulate_sparse_counts(
  landscape,
  beta = 0.00005,
  n0 = 150,
  n1 = 150,
  detection_threshold = 1,
  seed = 20,
  initial_population = 500
)
sim$sparsity
```

Fit the two-layer compiled pipeline.

```{r fit}
fit <- fit_alfak2(
  sim$counts,
  dt = 1,
  beta = 0.00005,
  min_cn = 1,
  max_cn = 4,
  local_shell_depth = 2,
  global_extra_shell = 1
)
head(summarize_alfak2(fit))
```

Inspect local and global support tiers.

```{r tiers}
table(summarize_alfak2(fit, layer = "local")$support_tier)
table(summarize_alfak2(fit, layer = "global")$support_tier)
```
